meaning: n.(犹太男子在祷告,学习,吃饭等时戴的)圆顶小帽
examples: Even though I'm an atheist, I still wear my yarmulke as it keeps my brain warm.
I don't know how I would have been received at some of these places had I worn a yarmulke or walked in holding hands with another man.
Max's even more dismal New York is cast in jumbled patterns of black, white and grey, until Mary sends Max a bright red pom-pom which he wears atop his yarmulke.
He comes to work every day dressed in a dark suit and a yarmulke.
Evanger's website touts its products by showing a photo of a dog wearing a yarmulke.
